transcript.interviewer: What do people get wrong about you when they first meet you?
transcript.interviewee: They think I have it all together. At work I'm the calm one, the person everyone brings problems to. That's the version they see.
transcript.interviewer: And under that calm one? Honestly? I'm exhausted. Being the capable one is a full-time job nobody knows I'm doing. I just don't let it show. What's below the exhaustion? There's a fear I don't say out loud. That the competence is the only thing anyone actually values. If I stopped being useful, I'm not sure anyone would stay. Is there anything underneath even that? This is the part I barely admit to myself. I think no one has ever actually met the person under all of it. I'm not even sure I have. The surface goes pretty far down before you'd hit me.
